problem.title: H-Index II
problem.description: Given an array of integers citations sorted in ascending order, where citations[i] is the number of citations a researcher received for their ith paper, return the researcher's h-index. Since the array is already sorted, achieve O(log n) time using binary search.
assumptions: citations is sorted in non-decreasing order.
h-index: largest h such that h papers have at least h citations each.
Must run in O(log n) time.

example.explanation: n=5. Binary search: for mid index i, there are n-i papers with >= citations[i] citations. If citations[i] >= n-i, answer is in left half. Otherwise in right half.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Binary Search [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Binary search on the index space. For a midpoint i in a sorted ascending array, there are n-i papers with at least citations[i] citations. If citations[i] >= n-i, the h-index is at least n-i, but we might do better by searching the left half. Otherwise search the right half. The key insight is that the answer n-i is monotonic, enabling binary search.
